_meta.purpose: Registry of the RECURRING publications we monitor for the BDBV 2026 outbreak, with each source's cadence, what it feeds, how it is archived, and how to recognize a freshly dropped edition. Companion to catalog.json (which lists the five DATA LEVERS) and to the snapshot freshness gate in snapshot_preflight.py. source_ingest.py reads this file to (a) report which recurring sources are due/overdue for a pull and (b) archive a pulled edition placed in the dropbox.
_meta.licensing: CC-BY-4.0 covers this schema and the Arcede annotations. Each publisher's content is under its own license (see per-source license field); confirm terms before redistribution.
_meta.dropbox: data/bundibugyo-2026/private/sources/ (gitignored). Drop a pulled PDF here, add a <filename>.meta.json sidecar with the extracted figures, then run: python3 source_ingest.py --ingest '<path>'.
_meta.staging_note: This is a STAGING / PROVENANCE layer. It never mutates a released snapshot. The released live-bdbv-2026-output.json and data/calibration-ledger.json are immutable; a new edition is incorporated only at a future scored refresh.

cadence_legend.ad_hoc: Published on events/updates, no fixed cadence; never marked overdue, only listed.
cadence_legend.continuous: Updated continuously; treated as always-current, never overdue.

archive_target_legend.poe_restricted_json: data/bundibugyo-2026/poe_traveler_counts.restricted.json provenance (mobility corroboration; not an outbreak count source).
archive_target_legend.external_covariate_metadata: data/external_sources/bdbv-2026.open-covariate-sources.json (source metadata and derived-only covariate provenance; not outbreak case-count truth).
archive_target_legend.geospatial_context_metadata: data/external_sources/bdbv-2026.open-covariate-sources.json (map/access context provenance; not outbreak case-count truth).

scheduled_prep_policy.timezone_basis: Cron expressions are UTC. Slot rationale names outbreak-local CAT (eastern DRC, UTC+2), Uganda EAT (UTC+3), Central European time for WHO/ECDC/Imperial, and US Eastern time for CDC/US-government pages.
scheduled_prep_policy.publication_boundary: Scheduled checks are source-freshness prep only. A same-day source can create a candidate review report, but release readiness remains governed by publication dates, source archiving, evidence chains, and the outbreak-local evening gate in release_snapshot.py.
scheduled_prep_policy.promotion_boundary: No scheduled check promotes source-review, watch-list, news, covariate, or context rows into scored counts. A source affects a snapshot only after byte archiving plus manifest/evidence-chain review.

slots.local_rationale: 07:15 CAT / 08:15 EAT catches overnight DRC MoH, Uganda MoH, and WHO-family updates before morning snapshot prep.
slots.publication_boundary: Prep report only; same-day findings stay candidate evidence until archived and reviewed.

slots.local_rationale: 12:45 CAT / 13:45 EAT catches ministry and WHO midday publication windows without waiting for US business hours.
slots.publication_boundary: Prep report only; official count changes trigger review but do not publish automatically.

slots.local_rationale: 18:30 CAT / 19:30 EAT aligns with the release-readiness evening boundary and late WHO/ECDC/Imperial Europe updates.
slots.publication_boundary: Feeds candidate-snapshot prep after the outbreak-local day is substantially complete; release remains manual.

slots.local_rationale: 17:15 US Eastern on weekdays catches CDC/US-government working-day updates while remaining late evening in Africa.
slots.publication_boundary: Cross-check/context prep only; CDC-derived changes need manifest/evidence review before scored use.

slots.local_rationale: 06:30 UTC Tuesday checks monthly/weekly humanitarian and open geospatial/covariate metadata once per week, enough for prep without over-polling.
slots.publication_boundary: Covariate/context metadata is never a case-count source and requires separate sensitivity review before model use.

sources.title: WHO AFRO Ebola BDBV Weekly External Situation Report
sources.publisher: World Health Organization, Regional Office for Africa
sources.next_expected: Report 02, data ~as of 2026-05-25 (weekly). PRIMARY refresh trigger: a new SitRep with a later data_as_of flips snapshot_preflight to READY.
sources.notes: Authoritative WHO line: per-health-zone suspected/confirmed/deaths, contacts, PoE/border notes. The single most important recurring source for a refresh. The HTML landing page and the PDF are archived as separate entries (suffixes -live and -pdf-*).

sources.title: ECDC Ebola DRC/Uganda outbreak page (weekly communicable-disease-threat update)
sources.publisher: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
sources.next_expected: Next weekly CDTR / outbreak-page refresh (typically Friday). Public bytes; archive under raw/<sha256>.
sources.notes: Independent cross-check line. The same outbreak page was updated 21 May 18:00 and is archived separately as ecdc-bdbv-drc-uga-2026-05-21-live.

sources.title: ECDC Threat Assessment Brief for the BDBV DRC/Uganda outbreak
sources.publisher: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc threat assessments. Public bytes; archive HTML/PDF if updated.
sources.notes: Risk and preparedness assessment. Count claims are WHO-derived and should remain cross-check/context, not primary count truth.

sources.title: US CDC Ebola Disease: Current Situation summary
sources.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.next_expected: Updated frequently; public domain, easy to byte-archive (raw/<sha256>).
sources.notes: Tracks confirmed/probable/suspected and 24-48h deltas; adds Nord-Kivu spread and probable-case category.

sources.title: CDC interim traveler public-health assessment and management guidance
sources.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc; CDC says guidance may be updated as new information becomes available.
sources.notes: Context-only travel/monitoring source. It defines affected countries and areas of concern; it is not a case-count source.

sources.title: CDC returning-travelers public information page
sources.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc public guidance; check when CDC travel measures change.
sources.notes: Context-only source for entry restrictions, enhanced screening, IAD rerouting, and 21-day monitoring.

sources.title: Imperial College MRC GIDA / WHO BDBV outbreak-size estimate updates
sources.publisher: Imperial College London, MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis (with WHO HEP/Uganda/AFRO)
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc; published on 18 May then a 20 May update. Carries the WHO PoE Table 3 (our PoE-count source) and the 400-900 true-size band. Watch for the next update.
sources.notes: Restricted (hash-recorded, raw bytes private). Method 2 CFR bands corrected to 26/33/40 and deaths 131 in the 20 May update. Supersession is recorded in normalized_content.supersedes.

sources.title: WHO Disease Outbreak News (DON) for this event
sources.publisher: World Health Organization
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc; a follow-up DON may be issued as the outbreak evolves.
sources.notes: Authoritative WHO Disease Outbreak News stream for this event. DON602 is the declaration anchor; DON603 is the 21 May official count/geography anchor. Public bytes.

sources.title: PAHO/WHO epidemiological alerts and updates
sources.publisher: Pan American Health Organization / World Health Organization
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc regional alerts. Added from the 21 May source sweep; retrieve manually if the website blocks automated fetches.
sources.notes: Official WHO regional alert for Member State preparedness. The 21 May PDF is hash-recorded/restricted in the outbreak manifest; promote count/geography claims only if the alert contains explicit figures and an evidence chain.

sources.title: Africa CDC outbreak / PHECS communications
sources.publisher: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc continental updates. Terms not confirmed; treat as restricted.
sources.notes: Continental-body line; spelling variant 'Mongwalu'.

sources.title: DRC Ministry epidemiological surveillance dashboard for Ebola Bundibugyo 2026
sources.publisher: Ministere de la Sante Publique, Hygiene et Prevoyance sociale, Democratic Republic of the Congo

api_request.query: query EpidemieDashboard { epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { name epidemiesFields { codeOms dateDebut statut souche } rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { edges { node { slug title reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication pdfOfficiel { node { mediaItemUrl } } situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } } } } }

sources.next_expected: Ad hoc dashboard updates. Archive the HTML shell and GraphQL response together; treat count semantics as source-review until the report table is confirmed as daily-delta versus cumulative.
sources.notes: Official DRC MoH surveillance dashboard. The 22 May SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 PDF cumulative Table IV is archived as the current model source-load table (83 DRC confirmed, 79 health-zone-attributed confirmed across 11 health zones, 4 confirmed samples without forms/unallocated). The 23 May pass exposed Sitrep/008 via GraphQL with dateRapportage 2026-05-22 and datePublication 2026-05-23, including Miti Murhesa in Sud-Kivu and Ituri zone details. Because no Sitrep/008 PDF URL was exposed at capture time and the structured table appears to be a daily/new-cases table, keep Sitrep/008 rows source-review until its report table is confirmed.

sources.title: IOM DTM Uganda Flow Monitoring Registry (cross-border movements)
sources.publisher: International Organization for Migration, Displacement Tracking Matrix
sources.next_expected: Monthly. FMR 9 (March) and FMR 10 (April) cover the epiweeks Imperial used; pull when indexed on ReliefWeb/DTM.
sources.notes: MOBILITY CORROBORATION ONLY, not an outbreak count source and not the source of the WHO PoE Table 3 counts. Provenance recorded in poe_traveler_counts.restricted.json _meta, not the outbreak manifest (it has no T1 provenance parent, so it does not fit the manifest tier schema). Confirms corridor structure (top DRC origins Beni>Mahagi>Bunia; top UGA destinations Kampala>Bundibugyo>Ntoroko>Nebbi>Kasese>Arua).

sources.title: HDX / Flowminder Democratic Republic of Congo: Population and Mobility Estimates
sources.publisher: Flowminder Foundation via Humanitarian Data Exchange
sources.next_expected: Monthly or ad hoc HDX resource refresh. Record package/resource metadata before any scored refresh; cache raw CSVs locally and publish only derived aggregate covariates unless the release explicitly carries CC BY attribution.
sources.notes: OPEN COVARIATE ONLY, not a case-count source and not evidence of outbreak spread. Use as a near-pre-outbreak March 2026 health-zone population/mobility baseline for population offsets, mobility-pressure covariates, and sensitivity-tested directed edge weights.

sources.title: HDX / HOT OSM DRC & Uganda Ebola Outbreak May 2026 OpenStreetMap Data
sources.publisher: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team via Humanitarian Data Exchange
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc outbreak map package updates. Keep ODbL attribution with any map or derived layer; do not mix raw ODbL database outputs into CC BY deliverables without an explicit license boundary.
sources.notes: GEOSPATIAL CONTEXT ONLY, not a case-count source and not evidence of outbreak spread. Use for operational roads, settlements, buildings, and access proxies with ODbL attribution and derived-data boundary controls.

sources.title: IFRC emergency appeal and press updates
sources.publisher: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc operational updates and appeal documents.
sources.notes: Operational response/readiness signal only. The 21 May press release mentions Bunia/Rwampara volunteers, regional spread concern, and Uganda Red Cross high alert; it is not a primary case/death count source. Publisher terms are not confirmed; archived bytes are hash-recorded/restricted rather than redistributed.

sources.title: WHO AFRO country/regional readiness articles tied to the BDBV outbreak
sources.publisher: World Health Organization, Regional Office for Africa
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc readiness articles; context-only unless they publish outbreak counts or geography.
sources.notes: Preparedness/surveillance capacity signal, not a case/death source.

sources.title: UK FCDO/UKHSA BDBV support and returning-worker monitoring updates
sources.publisher: UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office / UK Health Security Agency
sources.next_expected: Ad hoc response-support and traveler-monitoring updates.
sources.notes: Context-only response and traveler-monitoring source; not a primary outbreak count source.

sources.title: WHO Director-General official social updates for BDBV 2026
sources.publisher: World Health Organization Director-General
sources.next_expected: Continuous official social updates during the active outbreak. Use AIR or browser extraction to capture post text, URL/status id, publication timestamp, author verification context, and screenshot/hash when the platform blocks ordinary HTML fetch.
sources.notes: Official WHO leadership channel. May 23 update states Uganda reported three new confirmed cases, including a Ugandan health worker, a driver, and a Congolese national from Ituri for medical care, bringing Uganda confirmed positives to five. Treat as official candidate evidence, but still require byte/screenshot archive plus evidence-chain review before scored use.

sources.title: Uganda Ministry of Health official social updates
sources.publisher: Ministry of Health, Uganda

latest_known.edition: 2026-05-23 Uganda update statement reported by wire/news cross-checks

sources.next_expected: Continuous official ministry posts during the active outbreak. Prefer direct MoH website/PDF if available; otherwise use AIR or browser extraction for the social post and archive the extracted text plus screenshot/hash.
sources.notes: Official Uganda source channel for local case/status updates. Use as the primary Uganda channel once a post or press statement is captured; news reports about the statement remain cross-checks until the official post/PDF is archived.

sources.title: Radio Okapi BDBV outbreak reporting watch
sources.publisher: Radio Okapi
sources.next_expected: Continuous local reporting watch. Use AIR extraction for article text and keep findings in data/external_sources/bdbv-2026.watch.json unless independently confirmed by official sources.
sources.notes: Trusted local/UN-linked media signal with useful field networks, but not a primary case-count source. Never promote Radio Okapi-only claims into scored counts or corridor source load; use it to trigger official-source search and source-review handoffs.

sources.title: Wikipedia: 2026 Ituri Province Ebola epidemic (consensus aggregator)
sources.publisher: Wikipedia contributors
sources.next_expected: Continuous. Pin a specific oldid when archiving; it is the leading-edge consensus line the snapshot uses for the upper bound.
sources.notes: Archive by revision id (oldid). Never model_eligible when an official same-day metric exists (enforced in lovs_staged_observations).
